name: React
description: A JavaScript library for building user interfaces with a component-based architecture

description.value: A JavaScript library for building user interfaces with a component-based architecture

initial-release.comment: First public release (v0.3.0) on May 29, 2013 at JSConf US

active-maintenance.comment: Multiple releases in 2025, donated to React Foundation in October 2025

reactivity-model.comment: Uses Virtual DOM diffing for reconciliation

component-model.comment: Function components with hooks are preferred; class components still supported

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative views make code more predictable and easier to debug

state-management-approach.comment: Built-in useState/useReducer for local state; external libraries for complex global state

opinionatedness.comment: React is a library, not a framework - doesn't prescribe routing, state management, or project structure

backward-compatibility.comment: Strong commitment to gradual migration paths and deprecation warnings

primary-language.comment: Written in JavaScript (67.1% of repo), with Flow for type checking internally

typescript-support.comment: Excellent TypeScript support with @types/react; recommended by docs

template-syntax.comment: JSX/TSX is the primary templating syntax

styling-approach.comment: No built-in styling solution; works with CSS modules, CSS-in-JS, Tailwind, etc.

build-required.comment: Can use via CDN without build, but build tooling recommended for production

reactive-primitives.comment: useState, useRef hooks provide reactive primitives

two-way-binding.comment: One-way data flow; controlled components require explicit onChange handlers

event-handling.comment: onClick, onChange, etc. in JSX with synthetic event system

conditional-rendering.comment: Via ternary operators, && short-circuit, or if statements in JSX

list-rendering.comment: Via Array.map() in JSX with key prop

component-slots.comment: children prop and explicit named props for slots

teleport-portal.comment: createPortal() for rendering outside component tree

suspense.comment: Suspense component for async loading boundaries

context-provide-inject.comment: Context API (createContext, useContext) for dependency injection

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: useRef hook and ref prop for direct DOM access

official-router.comment: No first-party router; React Router is community standard

router-name.comment: Community standard, not first-party but widely adopted

route-guards.comment: Via React Router loaders and actions

built-in-state.comment: useState, useReducer for local component state

built-in-global-state.comment: Context API exists but not designed for frequent updates; external libs recommended

global-state-library.comment: Redux Toolkit is traditional; Zustand gaining popularity

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence; available via external libraries

official-cli.comment: Create React App deprecated; docs recommend meta-frameworks or Vite

hot-module-replacement.comment: Via bundlers like Vite, or meta-frameworks like Next.js

browser-devtools.comment: React DevTools browser extension for Chrome, Firefox, Edge

vs-code-extension.comment: Multiple extensions including ES7+ React snippets

error-messages.comment: Good error messages with component stack traces; suggestions for common mistakes

documentation-quality.comment: Excellent new react.dev docs with interactive examples and guides

playground.comment: CodeSandbox is commonly used; no official playground

bundle-size-core.comment: ~6.4 kB gzipped for react package alone

tree-shaking.comment: ES modules support tree shaking via bundlers

ssr-capable.comment: react-dom/server for SSR, typically via Next.js

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Benchmark score varies by implementation; typically 1.2-1.5x baseline

component-libraries.comment: Major libraries: MUI, Ant Design, Chakra UI, Radix, shadcn/ui, Mantine, Blueprint, React Bootstrap, Headless UI, Semantic UI, PrimeReact, NextUI, Fluent UI, Carbon, Theme UI

meta-framework.comment: Next.js is the primary meta-framework; also Remix, Gatsby

major-companies-using.value: Meta, Netflix, Airbnb, Uber, Amazon, Microsoft, Shopify, PayPal, Twitter

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Created by Meta; donated to React Foundation (Linux Foundation) in October 2025

job-market-demand.comment: Dominant in job market; most requested frontend skill globally

official-testing-library.comment: React Testing Library is community standard, not first-party

testing-library-name.comment: Community standard; Jest recommended in docs

pwa-support.comment: Via Vite PWA plugin or Workbox; no built-in but well supported

desktop-framework.comment: Works with Electron, Tauri for desktop apps

learning-curve.comment: Moderate learning curve; hooks and JSX require adjustment; simpler than Angular

concepts-to-learn.comment: JSX, Components, Props, State, Hooks (useState, useEffect, useContext), Virtual DOM, Reconciliation

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~5 minutes to set up with Vite and render first component

migration-path.comment: No official migration guides from other frameworks

incremental-adoption.comment: Can be added to existing pages; designed for incremental adoption
